title: Rebuild bigger is better
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild - bigger is better             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Mar 15, 2008 12:55 pm In the new Eva movies it seems as though they made the female cast more... voluptuous. Misato's boobs are bigger (this is really obvious in the first and last images especially) overall but Rei's seem to be the same size but more perky. It also looks as they either made Rei's hips larger or her waist smaller. Does anyone else reckon this has to do with fashion? In the nineties when Eva was made it was fashionable for girls to be skinny and have a boyish frame, are more curvaceous figures coming back into style now? This might tie into an article I read about anime and fashion following a 10 year economic cycle a while ago, it said something along the lines of that in the eighties with all the money floating around much more impressive Japanese animation was produced than what we see now (all those OVAs), futuristic design and big hair and boobs is what people wanted to see - whereas in the nineties with less money floating around the reverse occurred in Japanese animation (compare the look of Dirty Pair to Dirty Pair Flash) - which means the 00's should be the time when we get more decadent and OTT Japanese animation if it follows that 10 year cycle.

[]:             Quote Post by Furu » Sat Mar 15, 2008 1:39 pm I think you might be reading into it a bit too much. It could just be the effect of redrawing a 10+ year old TV series for the big screen with different artists, animators, and a much increased budget. Everyone looks different. Either that or they're consciously making things more fanservicey because, hey, it sells. I'll go with the first one for the benefit of the doubt, and also because it's a bit too subtle for the latter idea.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sat Mar 15, 2008 5:41 pm The one thing I'm confused about is why Eva still has fan-service. If Anno is against otakuism, then why have something thats a part of the life-style. Having people feel aroused for fictional characters seems a part of the life-style, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Mar 15, 2008 6:06 pm Fan service is as much part of anime as anything else. The Yebichu cans from the original is fan service, the A310 that Misato drives is fan service. Fan service doesn't necessarily have to be ecchi, nor is it something directed at Otakus. It's in there for the fans.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Mar 15, 2008 6:07 pm yeah, but a lot of it is "witty tongue in cheek" as opposed to just stupid; I mean for Gainax's flagship show, they practically never use the "Gainax bounce" they are infamous for

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Sat Mar 15, 2008 6:52 pm V wrote: yeah, but a lot of it is "witty tongue in cheek" as opposed to just stupid; I mean for Gainax's flagship show, they practically never use the "Gainax bounce" they are infamous for

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Mar 15, 2008 7:18 pm I see no size difference.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 15, 2008 7:21 pm UrsusArctos wrote: I see no size difference.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Mar 16, 2008 12:57 am Those two images are closer-up, that's why. No change otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 16, 2008 1:10 am No, the change really is there; you can even see it reflected in the folds of her shirt. "Look at the lines."

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Mar 16, 2008 2:29 am Misato had huge boobs to start with.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 16, 2008 4:38 am V wrote: yeah, but a lot of it is "witty tongue in cheek" as opposed to just stupid; I mean for Gainax's flagship show, they practically never use the "Gainax bounce" they are infamous for

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Mar 16, 2008 6:16 am slothen wrote: Misato had huge boobs to start with.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 16, 2008 10:06 am UrsusArctos wrote: I see no size difference.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Mar 16, 2008 12:57 pm why is everything in rebuild soft and fuzzy?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Mar 16, 2008 1:08 pm slothen wrote: why is everything in rebuild soft and fuzzy?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 16, 2008 1:13 pm But the colour scheme is A) Darker B) Warmer But yeah, not very good quality camrip (still a lot better than it could be)

[]:             Quote Post by Furu » Sun Mar 16, 2008 1:42 pm It'll be nice to watch Rebuild in about a month or so and have the Sachiel fight not look like an Angel is in a fierce life and death struggle against some floating neon green stripes.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Mar 16, 2008 2:03 pm Xard wrote: But the colour scheme is A) Darker B) Warmer

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Mar 16, 2008 2:18 pm Unless you are original source of camrip we have different versions

[]: Rebuild - bigger is better             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Mar 15, 2008 12:55 pm In the new Eva movies it seems as though they made the female cast more... voluptuous. Misato's boobs are bigger (this is really obvious in the first and last images especially) overall but Rei's seem to be the same size but more perky. It also looks as they either made Rei's hips larger or her waist smaller. Does anyone else reckon this has to do with fashion? In the nineties when Eva was made it was fashionable for girls to be skinny and have a boyish frame, are more curvaceous figures coming back into style now? This might tie into an article I read about anime and fashion following a 10 year economic cycle a while ago, it said something along the lines of that in the eighties with all the money floating around much more impressive Japanese animation was produced than what we see now (all those OVAs), futuristic design and big hair and boobs is what people wanted to see - whereas in the nineties with less money floating around the reverse occurred in Japanese animation (compare the look of Dirty Pair to Dirty Pair Flash) - which means the 00's should be the time when we get more decadent and OTT Japanese animation if it follows that 10 year cycle.

[]:             Quote Post by Furu » Sat Mar 15, 2008 1:39 pm I think you might be reading into it a bit too much. It could just be the effect of redrawing a 10+ year old TV series for the big screen with different artists, animators, and a much increased budget. Everyone looks different. Either that or they're consciously making things more fanservicey because, hey, it sells.
